Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to analyze a given temporal profile model for an insurance claim handling process identify anomalies generate hypotheses for why they exist and propose SQL queries to verify these hypotheses The output must be standalone not referencing the prompts instructions

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Temporal Profile Model R A 1 hr avg 10 min stdev Normal R E 1 day avg 8 hr stdev Normal R P about 1 point 04 days avg 1 hr stdev Anomaly Extremely low STDEV for a process that spans multiple steps indicating rigid artificial timing A C 2 hr avg 1 hr stdev Anomaly Very fast close after assign implying skipped intermediate steps like E or P E N 5 min avg 1 min stdev Anomaly Extremely fast transition from Evaluate to Notify skipping Approve or meaning Notify happens before Approve E C 1 hr avg 50 min stdev High variance but fast close after evaluate P N 7 days avg 2 days stdev Anomaly Massive delay from Approve to Notify If P is approve why wait 7 days to notify N C 30 min avg 5 min stdev Normal

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Identify Anomalies Task 1 Anomaly 1 R to P Receive to Approve Standard deviation is suspiciously low 1 hour relative to the average duration about 25 hours This suggests approvals are happening on a strict perhaps automated or batch processed schedule rather than organically based on claim complexity Anomaly 2 P to N Approve to Notify Extremely long average delay 7 days with high variance 2 days Notifying the customer should logically follow approval closely a 7 day delay suggests a severe bottleneck a disconnected system or a separate manual batch process for notifications Anomaly 3 A to C Assign to Close Quick closure 2 hours on average after assignment The standard process requires Evaluation and Approval This implies a significant portion of claims bypass the core steps and are closed prematurely Anomaly 4 E to N Evaluate to Notify Extremely short duration 5 minutes with low variance It is suspicious that notification happens almost immediately after evaluation potentially before the approval step occurs which could violate business rules notifying before approval

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Generate Hypotheses Task 2 For R to P Low STDEV Batch processing or automated approval for certain claim types For example claims under a specific amount might be auto approved at a specific time of day creating highly consistent timestamps For P to N Long Delay Notifications are handled by a different department or legacy system that runs on a weekly schedule Alternatively there is a backlog in the customer communication team For A to C Fast Closure Fraudulent fast closures duplicate claims being instantly rejected closed or specific low value claims being fast tracked without full evaluation approval For E to N Fast Execution The system automatically sends an interim notification upon evaluation completion or the Notify step is erroneously triggered by the evaluation system instead of the approval system

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Propose Verification SQL Queries Task 3 General note on SQL for temporal profiles Need to join claim_events to itself self join on claim_id to calculate time differences between activities Query 1 R to P Identify claims with suspiciously consistent approval times Find time differences between R and P Look for claims where the time diff is tightly clustered Join with claims to see if it correlates with claim_type or claim_amount Query 2 P to N Identify claims with excessively long approval to notification times Find claims where P to N is greater than 5 days Correlate with adjusters region or claims claim_type Query 3 A to C Identify prematurely closed claims missing E and P Find claims that have A and C but lack E or P Correlate with resource or adjuster Query 4 E to N Identify fast evaluate to notify claims Find claims where E to N is less than 10 minutes Check if P Approve happens after N indicating out of order execution

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Refine SQL Queries for PostgreSQL Use EXTRACT EPOCH FROM t2 timestamp minus t1 timestamp to get seconds Ensure proper aliases and clear logic

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Format the Output Ensure no reference to the prompt instructions Use clear headings for Anomalies Hypotheses and Verification SQL Queries

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction Review during drafting Did I include all tables claims adjusters claim_events Yes but adjusters is linked via resource Wait claim_events resource might be the adjuster name or ID but the schema does not explicitly map claim_events to adjusters directly unless resource equals adjuster_id or something I will assume claim_events resource can be joined to adjusters name or adjusters adjuster_id if needed or join claims claim_id to claim_events claim_id Wait there is no explicit adjuster_id in claims How do we know the adjuster The A Assign event resource column in claim_events likely identifies the adjuster I will use claim_events resource and join to adjusters name or adjusters adjuster_id assuming resource holds the ID or Name To be safe I will just use claim_events resource and note the join assumption or filter by adjusters region by joining claim_events where activity equals A to adjusters on resource equals adjuster_id Let us formulate the SQL carefully

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Polish Make sure the writing is crisp professional and directly addresses the three tasks Structure with clear headers No meta language about the prompt Ensure the PostgreSQL syntax is standard and correct Ensure assumptions like resource mapping to adjuster_id are briefly noted in the SQL comments if necessary but keep it clean Actually claim_events resource might just be an ID Let us just use it directly in grouping to avoid assumption errors or use a LEFT JOIN to adjusters safely This is safer and demonstrates the intent well
